hooks_predicted: Come explore the beautiful Jiuzhaigou National Park with me
The whole journey has been cut down from 8-10 hours to around 4 hours in total
Jiuzhaigou has been recognised as a World Heritage Site by UNESCO since 1992
It's most well known for its many waterfalls and colourful lakes which are especially stunning during autumn
In local folklore it's said that there's a monster in the lake
I had also lost my lens cap which fell off when I was trying to get off a super crowded bus
Jiuzhaigou brings together the worst and the best parts of travelling in China
China has so much to offer in terms of natural beauty which is one of the reasons why I keep on coming back here
selling_points_predicted: Journey time dramatically reduced from 8-10 hours to just 4 hours total with new high-speed railway station
UNESCO World Heritage Site recognized for its natural beauty since 1992
Famous for stunning waterfalls and colorful lakes, especially beautiful during autumn
Extensive and well-maintained infrastructure with 70km of boardwalks throughout the park
Convenient ticket booking for foreign travelers through trip.com using passport
Well-equipped park with numerous public toilets, rest points, restaurants and snack vendors
Affordable food and snacks despite being a tourist area
Perfect escape from hot city weather in Chengdu and Chongqing
Multiple stunning lakes and natural features to explore across three valleys
Worth visiting even with just one full day available
